text.title: OpenAI Chat Completions — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Chat Completions caller must enforce. Source: OpenAI Cookbook on rate limits, official rate-limits guide, Tier-based RPM/TPM tables (2026).

rule.statement: Read `response.choices[0].finish_reason` before parsing content. If it equals `"length"`, raise and retry with larger `max_tokens` — never parse truncated JSON.
rule.rationale: Truncated JSON silently corrupts downstream pipeline stages; the error surfaces in the consumer, not at the generation site.

rule.statement: Use `temperature` in [0.0, 0.2] for extraction, classification, or structured output. Use [0.7, 1.0] only for creative generation. Never leave the default for production.
rule.rationale: Default temperature is 1.0 which is wrong for >90% of pipeline use cases; explicit temperature pinning is required for reproducibility.

rule.statement: Default inner-loop calls to `gpt-4o-mini`. Escalate to `gpt-4o` only when an evaluation shows a measurable quality lift for that sub-task.
rule.rationale: gpt-4o is ~16× the input cost of gpt-4o-mini; defaulting to the flagship turns inner loops into the dominant cost line item.

rule.statement: Any call site issuing > 2 parallel requests uses `AsyncOpenAI` with a semaphore bounded by the per-model TPM budget — not unbounded `asyncio.gather`.

rationale.source: OpenAI Cookbook 'how to handle rate limits'

rule.rationale: Parallel async calls all draw from the same TPM bucket; unbounded fan-out hits 429 on the first burst.

rule.statement: Every call logs `model`, `usage.prompt_tokens`, `usage.completion_tokens`, `finish_reason`, latency, and a request id. Logs are queryable per pipeline run.
rule.rationale: Without per-call accounting, cost anomalies (image-heavy multimodal, runaway loops) surface only in monthly invoices.

rule.statement: Before dispatching a batch > 50 calls, inspect `x-ratelimit-remaining-requests` and `x-ratelimit-reset-requests` on the previous response. Hold the dispatch when remaining < 10% of budget.
rule.rationale: Rate limits are per-model. Pre-checking the header lets the dispatcher pause cheaply rather than burn retries on 429.
